title: Data Protection Risk Assessment Methodology
description: A structured methodology for identifying, assessing, and managing data protection risks aligned to the Malaysia Personal Data Protection Act 2010 (Act 709) and the 2024 Amendment Act (A1727). This methodology integrates with the PDPA compliance framework to ensure risk-based prioritization of data protection controls.
scope: All personal data processing activities subject to PDPA 2010, including collection, storage, use, disclosure, cross-border transfer, and disposal of personal data by data controllers and data processors.

steps.name: Context Establishment
steps.description: Define the scope, objectives, and criteria for the risk assessment. Identify all personal data processing activities, data flows, and data categories within scope.
activities: Identify personal data processing activities from the Records of Processing Activities (ROPA) under s138
Map data flows including collection points, storage locations, third-party processors, and cross-border transfers
Identify applicable PDPA sections, regulations, guidelines, codes of practice, and sector-specific requirements
Determine the risk appetite of the organization for data protection compliance
Identify stakeholders including data subjects, JPDP (Jabatan Perlindungan Data Peribadi), data processors, and management

steps.name: Risk Identification
steps.description: Systematically identify data protection risks arising from personal data processing activities, including threats to data subject rights, non-compliance with PDPA obligations, and security vulnerabilities.
activities: Review each processing activity against the 7 PDPA Principles for compliance gaps
Identify threats: unauthorized access, disclosure, loss, destruction, alteration of personal data
Assess cross-border transfer risks under s129 mechanisms
Review third-party processor arrangements for contractual and operational risks
Identify data subject rights fulfilment risks (access, correction, withdrawal, erasure, portability)
Consider sector-specific risks from applicable codes of practice
Review incident history and near-miss reports

steps.name: Risk Analysis
steps.description: Assess identified risks using the likelihood and impact scales to determine inherent risk levels. Consider both quantitative and qualitative factors.
activities: Assess likelihood of each risk occurring using the 5-point likelihood scale
Assess impact of each risk using the 5-point impact scale across all impact dimensions
Calculate inherent risk score (likelihood x impact)
Map inherent risk to the risk matrix to determine risk band
Document assumptions and evidence supporting each assessment

steps.name: Risk Evaluation
steps.description: Evaluate existing controls, determine residual risk, and prioritize risks for treatment based on residual risk level and organizational risk appetite.
activities: Identify and assess effectiveness of existing controls for each risk
Determine residual likelihood and impact after accounting for existing controls
Calculate residual risk score and map to risk matrix
Compare residual risk against risk appetite and tolerance thresholds
Prioritize risks requiring treatment based on residual risk level and PDPA compliance obligations

steps.name: Risk Treatment
steps.description: Select and implement appropriate risk treatment strategies. Note that certain PDPA obligations are mandatory and cannot be risk-accepted.
activities: Select treatment strategy: mitigate, transfer, avoid, or accept (where legally permissible)
Develop treatment plans with specific actions, owners, timelines, and success criteria
Implement controls aligned to the PDPA Controls Library (49 controls across 10 domains)
Validate that mandatory PDPA requirements are fully addressed regardless of risk level
Document risk treatment decisions and rationale

steps.name: Monitoring and Review
steps.description: Continuously monitor risk levels, control effectiveness, and changes to the processing environment that may affect the risk profile.
activities: Conduct quarterly reviews of the risk register
Monitor for changes in processing activities, data volumes, third-party arrangements, or regulatory requirements
Review and update risk assessments following data breaches, complaints, or JPDP enforcement actions
Track treatment plan progress and verify control implementation
Report risk status to senior management and the Data Protection Officer (DPO)

likelihoodScale.label: Rare
likelihoodScale.description: Event may occur only in exceptional circumstances. Adequate controls are in place and have been tested.

likelihoodScale.label: Unlikely
likelihoodScale.description: Event could occur in unusual circumstances. Controls exist but may not fully cover all scenarios.

likelihoodScale.label: Possible
likelihoodScale.description: Event could occur. Known vulnerabilities exist or controls have gaps that could be exploited.

likelihoodScale.label: Likely
likelihoodScale.description: Event will probably occur. Significant control gaps exist or similar events have occurred recently.

likelihoodScale.label: Almost Certain
likelihoodScale.description: Event is expected to occur. No effective controls in place, or event is already occurring.

impactScale.label: Negligible
impactScale.description: Minimal impact on data subjects or organization.
impactScale.dataSensitivity: Non-sensitive personal data only (e.g., business contact details)
impactScale.harmToIndividuals: No measurable financial, reputational, or physical harm to data subjects
impactScale.regulatoryPenalty: No JPDP interest; administrative guidance only
impactScale.organizationalReputation: No public awareness; internal issue only

impactScale.label: Minor
impactScale.description: Limited impact on a small number of data subjects; minor regulatory interest.
impactScale.dataSensitivity: General personal data (name, address, email, phone)
impactScale.harmToIndividuals: Minor inconvenience; temporary disruption; easily remediated
impactScale.regulatoryPenalty: JPDP advisory or warning letter; potential compounding of offence
impactScale.organizationalReputation: Limited local media coverage; minor stakeholder concern

impactScale.label: Moderate
impactScale.description: Significant impact on data subjects; JPDP investigation likely.
impactScale.dataSensitivity: Financial data, employment records, identification numbers
impactScale.harmToIndividuals: Financial loss (recoverable), reputational damage, stress and anxiety
impactScale.regulatoryPenalty: JPDP formal investigation; enforcement notice; fine up to RM300,000
impactScale.organizationalReputation: National media coverage; customer complaints; partner concern

impactScale.label: Major
impactScale.description: Severe impact on many data subjects; substantial regulatory enforcement expected.
impactScale.dataSensitivity: Sensitive personal data (health, religious belief, political opinion, criminal record) per s4 PDPA
impactScale.harmToIndividuals: Significant financial loss, identity theft risk, discrimination, psychological harm
impactScale.regulatoryPenalty: JPDP prosecution; fine up to RM500,000 and/or imprisonment up to 3 years; potential officer liability under s133A
impactScale.organizationalReputation: Widespread media coverage; significant customer churn; board-level escalation

impactScale.label: Critical
impactScale.description: Catastrophic impact on a large population; maximum regulatory penalties and potential criminal prosecution.
impactScale.dataSensitivity: Sensitive personal data including children's data, biometric data, health records
impactScale.harmToIndividuals: Severe financial loss, physical safety risk, long-term identity theft, irreversible harm
impactScale.regulatoryPenalty: JPDP maximum penalty: fine up to RM1,000,000 and/or 3 years imprisonment; corporate penalty up to RM3,000,000 or 10% annual Malaysian turnover (s130 A1727); officer personal liability
impactScale.organizationalReputation: International media coverage; regulatory investigations across jurisdictions; existential business impact

dpiaIntegration.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) Integration
dpiaIntegration.description: The PDPA (Amendment) Act 2024 introduces a mandatory DPIA requirement under s137 for high-risk processing activities. The DPIA process integrates with and extends this risk assessment methodology.
dpiaRequired: Large-scale processing of sensitive personal data (health, religious belief, political opinion, criminal record)
Systematic and extensive profiling with significant effects on data subjects
Large-scale monitoring of publicly accessible areas (CCTV, facial recognition)
Processing of children's personal data at scale
Cross-border transfer of personal data to countries without adequate protection
Use of new technologies that pose a high risk to data subject rights
Automated decision-making that significantly affects data subjects
Large-scale matching or combining of datasets from different sources
dpiaProcess: Describe the processing activity, its purposes, and its necessity under PDPA principles
Assess the necessity and proportionality of the processing in relation to the purpose
Identify and assess risks to data subject rights and freedoms using this methodology
Identify measures to mitigate risks, including safeguards, security measures, and mechanisms to protect data subjects
Document the assessment and its outcomes
Consult with the DPO and, where required, with the Commissioner before processing
Implement identified measures and integrate into the risk register
Review and update the DPIA when there are material changes to the processing
notes: DPIA is mandatory under s137 for prescribed high-risk processing — this is not risk-acceptable
DPIA results should be recorded in the risk register with cross-references to the DPIA report
The Commissioner may require a data controller to carry out a DPIA even where not otherwise mandatory
DPIA should be completed before the processing begins, not retrospectively
Where a DPIA indicates high residual risk, the data controller must consult the Commissioner under s137

reviewSchedule.fullAssessment: Annually, or following a significant change in processing activities
reviewSchedule.registerReview: Quarterly review of all risks and treatment plan progress
triggerEvents: New personal data processing activity introduced
JPDP enforcement action, complaint, or investigation
Change in applicable regulations, guidelines, or codes of practice
Introduction of new technology affecting personal data processing
